name: Name
errorPort: Port must be a number between 1 and 65535
noProxyHint: Add a server and turn it on
splitIntro: Pick which sites use the proxy. A domain also matches its subdomains.
splitBlacklistDesc: Listed sites skip the proxy; everything else uses it.
splitWhitelistDesc: Only listed sites use the proxy; everything else is direct.
aboutDescription: A lightweight extension for quick proxy switching: keep a list of servers, check their latency and turn the active one on with a single click. The choice survives browser restarts.
author: Author
faq1q: Ping says "offline" but the proxy works. Why?
faq1a: Extensions cannot open raw TCP connections, so ping is a heuristic based on the port response time. For SOCKS servers it can be wrong — what matters is whether traffic flows after you turn the proxy on.
faq2q: The proxy is on but pages will not load.
faq2a: Check the username and password and that the server is alive. In Chrome another extension (e.g. a VPN) can take over proxy control — disable it.
faq3q: Will the proxy stay on after a browser restart?
faq3a: Yes. The state is stored locally and re-applied automatically on startup.
faq4a: Nowhere. The server list and passwords live in the browser's local storage and never leave your device.
